text.title: Condensed Spec Writing Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for writing FR language, traceability, AC format, and Out of Scope in a condensed spec; antipatterns that indicate the feature has outgrown the condensed format.

rule.statement: Every Functional Requirement must use "System SHALL [specific, testable action]" language. Exclude ambiguous verbs: support, handle, manage, validate (when the validation rule is not specified).
rule.rationale: Ambiguous FRs produce ambiguous ACs. "System SHALL validate email" is testable only if the validation rule is stated — "System SHALL validate email format (RFC 5322)" is testable.

example.code: System SHALL allow registration with email + password
The system shall provide users with the ability to create a new account using their email address and a secure password that meets our security requirements
example.note: Verbose, ambiguous "security requirements". Rewrite as a short SHALL statement with explicit criteria.

rule.statement: Every FR must trace to exactly one User Story. Every AC must validate at least one FR. Orphaned FRs and orphaned ACs are quality gate failures.
rule.rationale: Traceability lets the executor agent verify coverage mechanically: every Must-priority FR has an AC that passes, therefore the feature satisfies its user stories.

rule.statement: Condense spec traceability to the FR table (Traces To column) and the US Acceptance Criteria field. No separate traceability matrix needed at condensed scale.
rule.rationale: A separate matrix adds overhead that the condensed format is designed to avoid. The table columns carry sufficient traceability for 3-5 FRs.

rule.statement: All ACs must use Given-When-Then format with specific values (not "valid email" but "test@example.com"). A condensed spec needs exactly one happy path AC and one error case AC.
rule.rationale: ACs without specific values cannot be mechanically verified. "User enters valid email" passes any input; "User enters test@example.com" passes only the correct input.

antipattern.description: AC items that read as requirements: "User can register with email and password."
antipattern.reason: Not a scenario — has no Given, When, Then. Cannot be verified as pass or fail. Rewrite as a Given-When-Then scenario.

rule.statement: If the agent produces more than 3 User Stories or more than 6 Functional Requirements, the feature is not "basic". Escalate to full spec-structure v2.0.
rule.rationale: A condensed spec with 8 FRs is not condensed — it is a full spec with missing sections. The missing sections (NFRs, detailed personas, Open Questions) will cause problems in later phases.

antipattern.description: Agents adding "Technical Notes" or "Architecture" sections to a condensed spec.
antipattern.reason: These belong in design.md. The spec answers WHAT and WHY only. Reject these sections at review.

antipattern.description: Omitting the Out of Scope section entirely.
antipattern.reason: Without explicit scope boundaries, any omitted feature becomes an implicit requirement. Out of Scope prevents implementation debates.
